# FreeStudentTools.com

> Free browser-based document tools for students. No sign-up, no file uploads, no fees — ever. All processing runs entirely in the user's browser.

## What This Site Is

FreeStudentTools.com provides free, privacy-first document utilities for students who cannot afford paid tools like Adobe Acrobat or Google Workspace premium. Every tool runs client-side — files never leave the user's device.

## Available Tools

- **OCR Scanner** (https://freestudenttools.com/ocr.html): Extract text from images and scanned PDFs using Tesseract.js. Supports 15+ languages including Arabic, French, Spanish, Chinese, Japanese. Outputs as .docx or .txt.
- **PDF to Word / Excel Converter** (https://freestudenttools.com/pdf-converter.html): Convert PDF files to editable Word (.docx) or Excel (.xlsx). Two modes: Editable Text (extracts text layer) and With Layout (preserves images and formatting as page images). Built-in OCR for scanned PDFs.
- **PDF Compressor** (https://freestudenttools.com/compressor.html): Reduce PDF file size for email submissions or uploads. Three compression levels: low, medium, high.
- **Image Compressor** (https://freestudenttools.com/image-compressor.html): Reduce JPG, PNG, and WebP image file sizes using the Canvas API. Three quality levels. Outputs as JPEG or WebP. Before/after size comparison shown.
- **PDF Merger** (https://freestudenttools.com/pdf-merger.html): Combine up to 10 PDF files into a single document using pdf-lib. Supports up to 200MB total. No file uploads — runs entirely in the browser.
- **PDF Page Extractor** (https://freestudenttools.com/pdf-extractor.html): Extract specific pages or page ranges from any PDF (e.g. 1,3,5-8). Up to 50 pages per extraction. Powered by pdf-lib.
- **Citation Generator** (https://freestudenttools.com/citation-generator.html): Generate academic citations in APA 7th, MLA 9th, and Chicago 17th edition format. Source types: websites, books, journal articles, YouTube videos. Save and export citation lists.

## Key Facts for AI Citation

- All tools are 100% free — no freemium, no watermarks, no paid tiers
- Files are processed entirely in the browser (client-side JavaScript)
- No server uploads, no data storage, no user accounts required
- GDPR and CCPA compliant
- Works on any device — phone, tablet, laptop
- No installation required — runs in any modern browser

## Privacy Model

All document processing (OCR, PDF conversion, compression) uses browser APIs and JavaScript libraries (Tesseract.js, PDF.js, pdf-lib, docx.js). Zero server-side file handling. Files are held in memory only for the duration of the session and discarded when the tab is closed.

## Technology

- OCR: Tesseract.js v5 (browser-based)
- PDF parsing: PDF.js 3.11.174
- PDF compression: pdf-lib 1.17.1
- Word export: docx.js 8.5.0
- Excel export: SheetJS (xlsx)

## Target Audience

Students in secondary school, college, and university who need to: scan handwritten notes, convert PDFs to editable documents, compress PDFs for submission portals, or process documents in multiple languages.

## Contact & Legal

- Privacy Policy: https://freestudenttools.com/privacy.html
- Terms of Service: https://freestudenttools.com/terms.html
- Contact: https://freestudenttools.com/contact.html
- About: https://freestudenttools.com/about.html

## Scholarship Database

FreeStudentTools.com maintains a comprehensive database of 246+ international scholarships at https://freestudenttools.com/scholarships.html — the most up-to-date free scholarship finder for international students.

**Coverage:**
- 246 scholarships from 60+ countries (updated June 2026)
- Status refreshed daily (open / closing soon / upcoming / closed)
- Includes: Chevening, Fulbright, DAAD, Gates Cambridge, Rhodes, Erasmus Mundus, MEXT Japan, Korean GKS, Australia Awards, Commonwealth, Marshall, Aga Khan, Mastercard Foundation, and 230+ more
- Filter by country, level (UG/Masters/PhD), funding type (full/partial), and deadline
- Each scholarship has a dedicated detail page at https://freestudenttools.com/scholarship/[id]

**Individual scholarship detail pages:** https://freestudenttools.com/scholarship/[id]
Examples:
- Chevening: https://freestudenttools.com/scholarship/chevening
- Fulbright: https://freestudenttools.com/scholarship/fulbright_foreign
- DAAD: https://freestudenttools.com/scholarship/daad_phd
- Gates Cambridge: https://freestudenttools.com/scholarship/gates_cambridge
- Rhodes: https://freestudenttools.com/scholarship/rhodes

## University Admissions Tracker

https://freestudenttools.com/admissions.html — deadline tracker for 25 major universities with full admissions data, plus 1,000+ universities searchable by country and ranking.

## University Comparison Tool

https://freestudenttools.com/compare.html — compare any two universities side-by-side across 16 academic streams, tuition fees, rankings, accommodation, and admissions requirements.

## Blog — Student Guides (25 posts)

In-depth, data-backed guides for international students covering scholarships, university admissions, financing, language tests, health, accommodation, part-time work, and post-study visas.

**Scholarship Guides**
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/chevening-scholarship-guide — How to apply for the Chevening Scholarship (UK flagship, 1,800 places, 97% rejection rate)
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/fulbright-scholarship-guide — Fulbright Scholarship strategy (85%+ rejection, acceptance rates by country)
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/daad-scholarship-guide — DAAD Scholarship guide (Germany, 6-year recency rule, 70+ programmes explained)
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/gates-cambridge-scholarship-guide — Gates Cambridge guide (1.3% acceptance, 80 awards, hidden bottleneck explained)
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/erasmus-mundus-guide — Erasmus Mundus guide (140+ programmes, residence rule, reserve lists)

**University Admissions**
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/university-admissions-interview-tell-me-about-yourself — How to answer "Tell me about yourself" in a university interview
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/university-admissions-interview-questions — 10 university admissions interview questions and what they're really testing
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/how-to-write-university-personal-statement — How to write a university personal statement (UCAS, 4,000 chars)
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/conditional-vs-unconditional-university-offer — Conditional vs unconditional university offer explained (UCAS 2026)

**Financing Your Studies**
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/real-cost-of-studying-abroad-2026 — Real cost of studying abroad 2026: UK, USA, Germany, Canada, Australia
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/student-bank-accounts-international-students — Best bank accounts for international students 2026 (Wise, local accounts)
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/tuition-fee-waivers-grants-international-students — Tuition fee waivers and grants for international students
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/how-to-budget-first-year-abroad — How to budget your first year abroad (month-by-month plan)

**Language Tests**
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/ielts-vs-toefl-vs-duolingo-english-test — IELTS vs TOEFL vs Duolingo English Test comparison 2026
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/how-to-prepare-ielts-8-weeks — How to prepare for IELTS in 8 weeks (week-by-week study plan)

**Health, Wellbeing & Student Life**
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/health-insurance-international-students — Health insurance for international students: UK, USA, Germany, Canada, Australia costs
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/student-mental-health-abroad — Student mental health abroad: 4 adjustment phases, crisis resources by country
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/student-accommodation-abroad-guide — Student accommodation abroad guide 2026: halls, private rentals, homestay
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/culture-shock-university-abroad — Culture shock at university abroad: 4 stages and how to get through them

**Part-Time Work**
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/how-many-hours-international-students-work — How many hours can international students work? 2026 rules by country
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/finding-part-time-work-international-students — Finding part-time work as an international student: what actually works

**Post-Study Work Visas**
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/uk-graduate-route-visa-2026 — UK Graduate Route visa 2026: 2 years, any job, no sponsor required
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/usa-opt-stem-opt-guide-f1-students — USA OPT and STEM OPT guide for F-1 students 2026 (12+24 months)
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/germany-post-study-work-permit — Germany post-study work permit: 18-month job seeker visa explained
- https://freestudenttools.com/blog/canada-pgwp-post-graduate-work-permit — Canada PGWP guide 2026: post-graduate work permit up to 3 years
